title: Context Engineering and Repository Grounding · Spiral Review
summary: Ground an agent in a large unfamiliar repository without flooding its context. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a repository context map and retrieval plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Rank repository files, official docs, generated artifacts, tests, and user statements by authority for the task.
Provide paths, search terms, dependency boundaries, and examples that route efficient discovery.
Separate one-turn constraints from durable repository instructions such as AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Source Authority evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Source Authority assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Rank repository files, official docs, generated artifacts, tests, and user statements by authority for the task. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Source Authority, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository context map and retrieval plan.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The first matching file is the source of truth. This fails the Source Authority contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompting Best Practices (Current Claude model guidance) — Controls clear instructions, examples, structure, reasoning, tools, and model-specific migration cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rank repository files, official docs, generated artifacts, tests, and user statements by authority for the task.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Context Refresh, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Context Refresh applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction.
